content.text:
<user_query>
Help me fix this Cursor rule: C:\Users\andre\.cursor\plugins\cache\cursor-public\redis-development\main\rules\_contributing.md

This rule is missing a description and file patterns, so it may never be applied. Please help me:
1. Add a meaningful description so the agent knows when to use this rule
2. Or add glob patterns to specify which files this rule should apply to
3. Or set alwaysApply: true if this rule should always be included

Please refer to https://cursor.com/docs/context/rules for the correct rule format.
</user_query>
I’ll inspect the current rule file and nearby rule examples, then patch `_contributing.md` to include valid metadata so Cursor can apply it reliably.

I found `_contributing.md` has no frontmatter at all, so you’re right it may be skipped. I’m adding Cursor-compatible frontmatter with a description plus targeted globs (and leaving `alwaysApply: false` so it only activates for rule-authoring files).

Updated `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\plugins\cache\cursor-public\redis-development\main\rules\_contributing.md` to include valid rule frontmatter so Cursor can apply it.

### What I changed
- Added a meaningful `description`
- Added `globs` so it triggers for rule-authoring files
- Set `alwaysApply: false` (scoped rule behavior)

New frontmatter now is:

```yaml
---
description: Guidelines for authoring Redis rule files with clear, actionable, example-driven content and measurable impact.
globs:
  - "**/rules/*.md"
  - "**/rules/_*.md"
alwaysApply: false
---
```

If you want this guidance to be loaded in every session regardless of file context, I can switch it to `alwaysApply: true` and remove `globs`.
